lib/pronyWorker.ts:        for(let i=0; i<N; i++) x[i] = maxE / N; // Start with decent uniform guess
lib/pronyWorker.ts:        
lib/pronyWorker.ts:        const AtA = new Array(N).fill(0).map(()=>new Float64Array(N));
lib/pronyWorker.ts:        const Atb = new Float64Array(N);
lib/pronyWorker.ts:        
lib/pronyWorker.ts:        for(let i=0; i<N; i++){
lib/pronyWorker.ts:            for(let j=0; j<N; j++){
lib/pronyWorker.ts:                for(let k=0; k<2*M; k++){
lib/pronyWorker.ts:                    AtA[i][j] += A[k][i] * A[k][j];
lib/pronyWorker.ts:                }
lib/pronyWorker.ts:            }
lib/pronyWorker.ts:            for(let k=0; k<2*M; k++){
lib/pronyWorker.ts:                Atb[i] += A[k][i] * b[k];
lib/pronyWorker.ts:            }
lib/pronyWorker.ts:        }
lib/pronyWorker.ts:        
lib/pronyWorker.ts:        // Tikhonov Regularization to prevent singular matrix / smooth terms
lib/pronyWorker.ts:        let maxDiag = 0;
lib/pronyWorker.ts:        for(let i=0; i<N; i++) maxDiag = Math.max(maxDiag, AtA[i][i]);
lib/pronyWorker.ts:        const lambda = 1e-4 * maxDiag;
lib/pronyWorker.ts:        for(let i=0; i<N; i++){
lib/pronyWorker.ts:            AtA[i][i] += lambda;
lib/pronyWorker.ts:        }
lib/pronyWorker.ts:        
lib/pronyWorker.ts:        // Projected Gradient Descent for Non-negative Least Squares
lib/pronyWorker.ts:        const maxIter = 100000;
lib/pronyWorker.ts:        const lr = maxDiag > 0 ? 0.05 / maxDiag : 1e-3;
lib/pronyWorker.ts:
lib/pronyWorker.ts:        for(let iter=0; iter<maxIter; iter++){
lib/pronyWorker.ts:            let diff = 0;
lib/pronyWorker.ts:            const grad = new Float64Array(N);
lib/pronyWorker.ts:            for(let i=0; i<N; i++){
lib/pronyWorker.ts:                for(let j=0; j<N; j++){
lib/pronyWorker.ts:                    grad[i] += AtA[i][j] * x[j];
lib/pronyWorker.ts:                }
lib/pronyWorker.ts:                grad[i] -= Atb[i];
lib/pronyWorker.ts:            }
lib/pronyWorker.ts:            for(let i=0; i<N; i++){
lib/pronyWorker.ts:                const old = x[i];
lib/pronyWorker.ts:                x[i] = Math.max(0, x[i] - lr * grad[i]);
lib/pronyWorker.ts:                diff += Math.abs(x[i] - old);
lib/pronyWorker.ts:            }
lib/pronyWorker.ts:            if(diff < 1e-8) break;
lib/pronyWorker.ts:        }
lib/pronyWorker.ts:        
lib/pronyWorker.ts:        const E_inf = x[0];
lib/pronyWorker.ts:        const terms = [];
lib/pronyWorker.ts:        let E_sum = E_inf;
lib/pronyWorker.ts:        for(let i=0; i<numTerms; i++) {
lib/pronyWorker.ts:            terms.push({ tau: tau[i], E: x[1+i] });
lib/pronyWorker.ts:            E_sum += x[1+i];
lib/pronyWorker.ts:        }
lib/pronyWorker.ts:        
lib/pronyWorker.ts:        const points = [];
lib/pronyWorker.ts:        let sse = 0;
lib/pronyWorker.ts:        for(let j=0; j<M; j++){
lib/pronyWorker.ts:            let st_fit = E_inf;
lib/pronyWorker.ts:            let ls_fit = 0;
lib/pronyWorker.ts:            const w = data[j].omega;
lib/pronyWorker.ts:            for(let i=0; i<numTerms; i++){
lib/pronyWorker.ts:                const t = tau[i];
lib/pronyWorker.ts:                const E = x[1+i];
lib/pronyWorker.ts:                const wt = w * t;
lib/pronyWorker.ts:                const wt2 = wt * wt;
lib/pronyWorker.ts:                const denom = 1 + wt2;
lib/pronyWorker.ts:                st_fit += E * wt2 / denom;
lib/pronyWorker.ts:                ls_fit += E * wt / denom;
lib/pronyWorker.ts:            }
lib/pronyWorker.ts:            sse += Math.pow(st_fit - data[j].storage, 2) + Math.pow(ls_fit - data[j].loss, 2);
lib/pronyWorker.ts:            points.push({
lib/pronyWorker.ts:                omega: data[j].omega,
lib/pronyWorker.ts:                storage: data[j].storage,
lib/pronyWorker.ts:                loss: data[j].loss,
lib/pronyWorker.ts:                storage_fit: st_fit,
lib/pronyWorker.ts:                loss_fit: ls_fit
lib/pronyWorker.ts:            });
lib/pronyWorker.ts:        }
lib/pronyWorker.ts:        
lib/pronyWorker.ts:        // Sort terms by relaxation time tau
lib/pronyWorker.ts:        terms.sort((a,b)=>a.tau - b.tau);
lib/pronyWorker.ts:        
lib/pronyWorker.ts:        let abaqusStr = "*VISCOELASTIC, TIME=PRONY\n";
lib/pronyWorker.ts:        terms.forEach(t => {
lib/pronyWorker.ts:            if (t.E > 1e-10) {
lib/pronyWorker.ts:                const ratio = t.E / E_sum;
lib/pronyWorker.ts:                abaqusStr += `${(ratio).toExponential(5)}, ${(ratio).toExponential(5)}, ${t.tau.toExponential(5)}\n`;
lib/pronyWorker.ts:            }
lib/pronyWorker.ts:        });
lib/pronyWorker.ts:        
lib/pronyWorker.ts:        self.postMessage({
lib/pronyWorker.ts:            type: 'PRONY_RESULT',
lib/pronyWorker.ts:            payload: {
lib/pronyWorker.ts:                E_inf,
lib/pronyWorker.ts:                E_sum,
lib/pronyWorker.ts:                terms: terms.filter(t => t.E > 1e-10),
lib/pronyWorker.ts:                points,
lib/pronyWorker.ts:                error_metric: Math.sqrt(sse / (2*M)),
lib/pronyWorker.ts:                abaqusCard: abaqusStr
lib/pronyWorker.ts:            }
lib/pronyWorker.ts:        });
lib/pronyWorker.ts:        
lib/pronyWorker.ts:    } catch(e) {
lib/pronyWorker.ts:        self.postMessage({
lib/pronyWorker.ts:            type: 'ERROR',
lib/pronyWorker.ts:            error: e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e)
lib/pronyWorker.ts:        });
lib/pronyWorker.ts:    }
lib/pronyWorker.ts:}
lib/math/convexHull.ts:export interface Point2D {
lib/math/convexHull.ts:  x: number;
lib/math/convexHull.ts:  y: number;
lib/math/convexHull.ts:  id?: string;
lib/math/convexHull.ts:  data?: any;
lib/math/convexHull.ts:}
lib/math/convexHull.ts:
lib/math/convexHull.ts:// CCW > 0 means counter-clockwise (left turn)
lib/math/convexHull.ts:// CCW < 0 means clockwise (right turn)
lib/math/convexHull.ts:// CCW = 0 means collinear
lib/math/convexHull.ts:function ccw(p1: Point2D, p2: Point2D, p3: Point2D): number {
lib/math/convexHull.ts:  return (p2.x - p1.x) * (p3.y - p1.y) - (p2.y - p1.y) * (p3.x - p1.x);
lib/math/convexHull.ts:}
lib/math/convexHull.ts:
lib/math/convexHull.ts:export function grahamScan(points: Point2D[]): Point2D[] {
lib/math/convexHull.ts:  if (points.length <= 3) return points;
lib/math/convexHull.ts:
lib/math/convexHull.ts:  // Find the point with the lowest y-coordinate (and leftmost if tied)
lib/math/convexHull.ts:  let lowest = points[0];
lib/math/convexHull.ts:  let lowestIdx = 0;
lib/math/convexHull.ts:  for (let i = 1; i < points.length; i++) {
lib/math/convexHull.ts:    if (points[i].y < lowest.y || (points[i].y === lowest.y && points[i].x < lowest.x)) {
lib/math/convexHull.ts:      lowest = points[i];
lib/math/convexHull.ts:      lowestIdx = i;
lib/math/convexHull.ts:    }
lib/math/convexHull.ts:  }
lib/math/convexHull.ts:
lib/math/convexHull.ts:  // Swap lowest to the first position
lib/math/convexHull.ts:  const temp = points[0];
lib/math/convexHull.ts:  points[0] = points[lowestIdx];
lib/math/convexHull.ts:  points[lowestIdx] = temp;
lib/math/convexHull.ts:
lib/math/convexHull.ts:  // Sort remaining points by polar angle with respect to the lowest point
lib/math/convexHull.ts:  const sorted = points.slice(1).sort((a, b) => {
lib/math/convexHull.ts:    const angle = ccw(lowest, a, b);
lib/math/convexHull.ts:    if (angle === 0) {
lib/math/convexHull.ts:      // collinear, closer point first
lib/math/convexHull.ts:      const distA = (a.x - lowest.x) ** 2 + (a.y - lowest.y) ** 2;
lib/math/convexHull.ts:      const distB = (b.x - lowest.x) ** 2 + (b.y - lowest.y) ** 2;
lib/math/convexHull.ts:      return distA - distB;
lib/math/convexHull.ts:    }
lib/math/convexHull.ts:    return angle > 0 ? -1 : 1;
lib/math/convexHull.ts:  });
lib/math/convexHull.ts:
lib/math/convexHull.ts:  const hull: Point2D[] = [lowest, sorted[0]];
lib/math/convexHull.ts:
lib/math/convexHull.ts:  for (let i = 1; i < sorted.length; i++) {
lib/math/convexHull.ts:    let top = hull.length - 1;
lib/math/convexHull.ts:    while (hull.length >= 2 && ccw(hull[top - 1], hull[top], sorted[i]) <= 0) {
lib/math/convexHull.ts:      hull.pop();
lib/math/convexHull.ts:      top--;
lib/math/convexHull.ts:    }
lib/math/convexHull.ts:    hull.push(sorted[i]);
lib/math/convexHull.ts:  }
lib/math/convexHull.ts:
lib/math/convexHull.ts:  return hull;
lib/math/convexHull.ts:}
lib/math/pca.ts:export class PCA {
lib/math/pca.ts:  // Simple NIPALS algorithm for PCA (Non-linear Iterative Partial Least Squares)
lib/math/pca.ts:  // Extracts the first numComponents principal components.
lib/math/pca.ts:  
lib/math/pca.ts:  static getComponents(data: number[][], numComponents: number = 2) {
lib/math/pca.ts:      if (!data.length || !data[0].length) return { projected: [], loadingVectors: [] };
lib/math/pca.ts:      
lib/math/pca.ts:      const n = data.length;
lib/math/pca.ts:      const m = data[0].length;
lib/math/pca.ts:      
lib/math/pca.ts:      // 1. Zero-mean the data
lib/math/pca.ts:      const means = new Array(m).fill(0);
lib/math/pca.ts:      for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) {
lib/math/pca.ts:          for (let j = 0; j < m; j++) {
lib/math/pca.ts:              means[j] += data[i][j];
lib/math/pca.ts:          }
lib/math/pca.ts:      }
lib/math/pca.ts:      for (let j = 0; j < m; j++) {
lib/math/pca.ts:          means[j] /= n;
lib/math/pca.ts:      }
lib/math/pca.ts:      
lib/math/pca.ts:      const X = data.map(row => row.map((val, j) => val - means[j]));
lib/math/pca.ts:      
lib/math/pca.ts:      const loadingVectors: number[][] = [];
lib/math/pca.ts:      const scores: number[][] = new Array(n).fill(0).map(() => new Array(numComponents).fill(0));
lib/math/pca.ts:      
lib/math/pca.ts:      const residualX = X.map(row => [...row]);
lib/math/pca.ts:      
lib/math/pca.ts:      for (let k = 0; k < Math.min(numComponents, m); k++) {
lib/math/pca.ts:          // Initialize score vector t as the first column of residualX
lib/math/pca.ts:          const t = residualX.map(row => row[0]);
lib/math/pca.ts:          const p = new Array(m).fill(0);
lib/math/pca.ts:          
lib/math/pca.ts:          let iter = 0;
lib/math/pca.ts:          let diff = 1;
lib/math/pca.ts:          while (iter < 100 && diff > 1e-6) {
lib/math/pca.ts:              const oldT = [...t];
lib/math/pca.ts:              
lib/math/pca.ts:              // p = X^T t / (t^T t)
lib/math/pca.ts:              let tTt = 0;
lib/math/pca.ts:              for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) tTt += t[i] * t[i];
lib/math/pca.ts:              
lib/math/pca.ts:              if (tTt === 0) break; // Zero variance
lib/math/pca.ts:              
lib/math/pca.ts:              for (let j = 0; j < m; j++) {
lib/math/pca.ts:                  let sum = 0;
lib/math/pca.ts:                  for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) {
lib/math/pca.ts:                      sum += residualX[i][j] * t[i];
lib/math/pca.ts:                  }
lib/math/pca.ts:                  p[j] = sum / tTt;
lib/math/pca.ts:              }
lib/math/pca.ts:              
lib/math/pca.ts:              // Normalize p: p = p / ||p||
lib/math/pca.ts:              let pNorm = 0;
lib/math/pca.ts:              for (let j = 0; j < m; j++) pNorm += p[j] * p[j];
lib/math/pca.ts:              pNorm = Math.sqrt(pNorm);
lib/math/pca.ts:              if (pNorm === 0) break;
lib/math/pca.ts:              for (let j = 0; j < m; j++) p[j] /= pNorm;
lib/math/pca.ts:              
lib/math/pca.ts:              // t = X p / (p^T p) = X p  (since p^T p = 1)
lib/math/pca.ts:              for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) {
lib/math/pca.ts:                  let sum = 0;
lib/math/pca.ts:                  for (let j = 0; j < m; j++) {
lib/math/pca.ts:                      sum += residualX[i][j] * p[j];
lib/math/pca.ts:                  }
lib/math/pca.ts:                  t[i] = sum;
lib/math/pca.ts:              }
lib/math/pca.ts:              
lib/math/pca.ts:              // Check convergence
lib/math/pca.ts:              diff = 0;
lib/math/pca.ts:              for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) {
lib/math/pca.ts:                  diff += Math.pow(t[i] - oldT[i], 2);
lib/math/pca.ts:              }
lib/math/pca.ts:              iter++;
lib/math/pca.ts:          }
lib/math/pca.ts:          
lib/math/pca.ts:          loadingVectors.push([...p]);
lib/math/pca.ts:          for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) {
lib/math/pca.ts:              scores[i][k] = t[i];
lib/math/pca.ts:          }
lib/math/pca.ts:          
lib/math/pca.ts:          // Deflate: X = X - t p^T
lib/math/pca.ts:          for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) {
lib/math/pca.ts:              for (let j = 0; j < m; j++) {
lib/math/pca.ts:                  residualX[i][j] -= t[i] * p[j];
lib/math/pca.ts:              }
lib/math/pca.ts:          }
lib/math/pca.ts:      }
lib/math/pca.ts:      
lib/math/pca.ts:      return { projected: scores, loadingVectors };
lib/math/pca.ts:  }
lib/math/pca.ts:}
lib/kdeWorker.ts:export interface KdeMessage {
lib/kdeWorker.ts:  type: 'CALCULATE_KDE';
lib/kdeWorker.ts:  payload: {
lib/kdeWorker.ts:    points: {x: number, y: number}[];
lib/kdeWorker.ts:    gridSize?: number; // e.g. 50x50
lib/kdeWorker.ts:  };
lib/kdeWorker.ts:}
lib/kdeWorker.ts:
lib/kdeWorker.ts:export interface KdeResponse {
lib/kdeWorker.ts:  type: 'KDE_CALCULATED' | 'ERROR';
lib/kdeWorker.ts:  payload?: {
lib/kdeWorker.ts:    grid: {x: number, y: number, z: number}[];
lib/kdeWorker.ts:    minX: number;
lib/kdeWorker.ts:    maxX: number;
lib/kdeWorker.ts:    minY: number;
lib/kdeWorker.ts:    maxY: number;
lib/kdeWorker.ts:    minZ: number;
lib/kdeWorker.ts:    maxZ: number;
lib/kdeWorker.ts:  };
lib/kdeWorker.ts:  error?: string;
lib/kdeWorker.ts:}
lib/kdeWorker.ts:
lib/kdeWorker.ts:// 2D Gaussian Kernel
lib/kdeWorker.ts:function gaussianKernel(dSquared: number, bandwidthSquared: number): number {
lib/kdeWorker.ts:  return Math.exp(-dSquared / (2 * bandwidthSquared)) / (2 * Math.PI * bandwidthSquared);
lib/kdeWorker.ts:}
lib/kdeWorker.ts:
lib/kdeWorker.ts:// Scott's rule of thumb
lib/kdeWorker.ts:function calculateBandwidth(values: number[]): number {
lib/kdeWorker.ts:  if (values.length < 2) return 1;
lib/kdeWorker.ts:  const n = values.length;
lib/kdeWorker.ts:  const mean = values.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / n;
lib/kdeWorker.ts:  const variance = values.reduce((a, b) => a + Math.pow(b - mean, 2), 0) / (n - 1);
lib/kdeWorker.ts:  return 1.06 * Math.sqrt(variance) * Math.pow(n, -1/5);
lib/kdeWorker.ts:}
lib/kdeWorker.ts:
lib/kdeWorker.ts:self.onmessage = (e: MessageEvent<KdeMessage>) => {
lib/kdeWorker.ts:  try {
lib/kdeWorker.ts:    const { points, gridSize = 50 } = e.data.payload;
lib/kdeWorker.ts:    if (points.length === 0) throw new Error("No points for KDE");
lib/kdeWorker.ts:
lib/kdeWorker.ts:    let minX = Infinity, maxX = -Infinity;
lib/kdeWorker.ts:    let minY = Infinity, maxY = -Infinity;
lib/kdeWorker.ts:    
lib/kdeWorker.ts:    const xVals = [];
lib/kdeWorker.ts:    const yVals = [];
lib/kdeWorker.ts:    
lib/kdeWorker.ts:    for (const p of points) {
lib/kdeWorker.ts:      if (p.x < minX) minX = p.x;
lib/kdeWorker.ts:      if (p.x > maxX) maxX = p.x;
lib/kdeWorker.ts:      if (p.y < minY) minY = p.y;
lib/kdeWorker.ts:      if (p.y > maxY) maxY = p.y;
lib/kdeWorker.ts:      xVals.push(p.x);
lib/kdeWorker.ts:      yVals.push(p.y);
lib/kdeWorker.ts:    }
lib/kdeWorker.ts:    
lib/kdeWorker.ts:    // Add margin
lib/kdeWorker.ts:    const diffX = maxX - minX || 1;
lib/kdeWorker.ts:    const diffY = maxY - minY || 1;
lib/kdeWorker.ts:    minX -= diffX * 0.1;
lib/kdeWorker.ts:    maxX += diffX * 0.1;
lib/kdeWorker.ts:    minY -= diffY * 0.1;
lib/kdeWorker.ts:    maxY += diffY * 0.1;
lib/kdeWorker.ts:    
lib/kdeWorker.ts:    const bwX = calculateBandwidth(xVals) || (diffX * 0.1);
lib/kdeWorker.ts:    const bwY = calculateBandwidth(yVals) || (diffY * 0.1);
lib/kdeWorker.ts:    const bwSq = Math.max(bwX * bwY, Number.MIN_VALUE); // simplified isotropic approximation for weight
lib/kdeWorker.ts:
lib/kdeWorker.ts:    const grid = [];
lib/kdeWorker.ts:    let minZ = Infinity, maxZ = -Infinity;
lib/kdeWorker.ts:
lib/kdeWorker.ts:    for (let j = 0; j < gridSize; j++) {
lib/kdeWorker.ts:      const cy = minY + (j / (gridSize - 1)) * (maxY - minY);
lib/kdeWorker.ts:      for (let i = 0; i < gridSize; i++) {
lib/kdeWorker.ts:        const cx = minX + (i / (gridSize - 1)) * (maxX - minX);
lib/kdeWorker.ts:        
lib/kdeWorker.ts:        let z = 0;
lib/kdeWorker.ts:        for (const p of points) {
lib/kdeWorker.ts:           // scaled distance squared to treat x and y equally in kernel footprint
lib/kdeWorker.ts:           const dx = (p.x - cx) / (bwX || 1);
lib/kdeWorker.ts:           const dy = (p.y - cy) / (bwY || 1);
lib/kdeWorker.ts:           const dSq = dx*dx + dy*dy;
lib/kdeWorker.ts:           z += Math.exp(-dSq / 2); // 2D standard normal
lib/kdeWorker.ts:        }
lib/kdeWorker.ts:        z = z / points.length; // normalize
lib/kdeWorker.ts:        
lib/kdeWorker.ts:        if (z < minZ) minZ = z;
lib/kdeWorker.ts:        if (z > maxZ) maxZ = z;
lib/kdeWorker.ts:        grid.push({ x: cx, y: cy, z });
lib/kdeWorker.ts:      }
lib/kdeWorker.ts:    }
lib/kdeWorker.ts:
lib/kdeWorker.ts:    self.postMessage({
lib/kdeWorker.ts:      type: 'KDE_CALCULATED',
lib/kdeWorker.ts:      payload: { grid, minX, maxX, minY, maxY, minZ, maxZ }
lib/kdeWorker.ts:    } as KdeResponse);
lib/kdeWorker.ts:
lib/kdeWorker.ts:  } catch (error) {
lib/kdeWorker.ts:    self.postMessage({
lib/kdeWorker.ts:      type: 'ERROR',
lib/kdeWorker.ts:      error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)
lib/kdeWorker.ts:    });
lib/kdeWorker.ts:  }
lib/kdeWorker.ts:};
lib/sobolWorker.ts:import { FormulaConfig, Product, PropertyValue } from '../types';
lib/sobolWorker.ts:import { formulaEngine } from './formulaParser';
lib/sobolWorker.ts:
lib/sobolWorker.ts:export interface SobolMessage {
lib/sobolWorker.ts:  type: 'RUN_SOBOL';
lib/sobolWorker.ts:  payload: {
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    targetFormulaId: string;
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    formulas: FormulaConfig[];
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    product: Product;
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    variances: Record<string, number>; // std dev as % of base value (e.g., 5 for 5%)
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    iterations?: number; // N, total samples will be N * (D + 2)
lib/sobolWorker.ts:  };
lib/sobolWorker.ts:}
lib/sobolWorker.ts:
lib/sobolWorker.ts:export interface SobolResponse {
lib/sobolWorker.ts:  type: 'SOBOL_COMPLETE' | 'ERROR';
lib/sobolWorker.ts:  payload?: {
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    firstOrder: { name: string; value: number }[];
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    totalEffect: { name: string; value: number }[];
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    interactions: { name: string; value: number }[]; // Total - FirstOrder
lib/sobolWorker.ts:  };
lib/sobolWorker.ts:  error?: string;
lib/sobolWorker.ts:}
lib/sobolWorker.ts:
lib/sobolWorker.ts:// Box-Muller transform for normal distribution sampling
lib/sobolWorker.ts:function randomNormal(mean: number, stdDev: number): number {
lib/sobolWorker.ts:  let u = 0, v = 0;
lib/sobolWorker.ts:  while(u === 0) u = Math.random(); //Converting [0,1) to (0,1)
lib/sobolWorker.ts:  while(v === 0) v = Math.random();
lib/sobolWorker.ts:  const num = Math.sqrt( -2.0 * Math.log( u ) ) * Math.cos( 2.0 * Math.PI * v );
lib/sobolWorker.ts:  return num * stdDev + mean;
lib/sobolWorker.ts:}
lib/sobolWorker.ts:
lib/sobolWorker.ts:self.onmessage = (e: MessageEvent<SobolMessage>) => {
lib/sobolWorker.ts:  try {
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    const { targetFormulaId, formulas, product, variances, iterations = 2000 } = e.data.payload;
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    const evaluator = formulaEngine.compileGraph(formulas);
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    // Determine which properties actually need variance (inputs for Sobol)
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    const baseProperties = product.properties;
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    const inputKeys: string[] = [];
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    const inputMeans: number[] = [];
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    const inputStdDevs: number[] = [];
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    for (const [key, val] of Object.entries(baseProperties)) {
lib/sobolWorker.ts:        const numVal = parseFloat(String(val.value));
lib/sobolWorker.ts:        if (!isNaN(numVal) && variances[key] && variances[key] > 0) {
lib/sobolWorker.ts:            inputKeys.push(key);
lib/sobolWorker.ts:            inputMeans.push(numVal);
lib/sobolWorker.ts:            inputStdDevs.push(numVal * (variances[key] / 100));
lib/sobolWorker.ts:        }
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    }
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    const D = inputKeys.length;
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    if (D === 0) {
lib/sobolWorker.ts:        throw new Error("No variables with variance > 0 provided for Sobol analysis.");
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    }
lib/sobolWorker.ts:
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    const N = iterations;
lib/sobolWorker.ts:
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    // Helper to evaluate model from an array of inputs matching inputKeys
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    const evaluate = (inputs: number[]): number => {
lib/sobolWorker.ts:        const perturbedProps: Record<string, PropertyValue> = { ...baseProperties };
lib/sobolWorker.ts:        for (let i = 0; i < D; i++) {
lib/sobolWorker.ts:            const key = inputKeys[i];
lib/sobolWorker.ts:            perturbedProps[key] = { ...perturbedProps[key], value: inputs[i] };
lib/sobolWorker.ts:        }
lib/sobolWorker.ts:        const testProduct = { ...product, properties: perturbedProps } as Product;
lib/sobolWorker.ts:        return evaluator(testProduct)[targetFormulaId] || 0;
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    };
lib/sobolWorker.ts:
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    // Generate A and B matrices (N x D)
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    const A: number[][] = [];
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    const B: number[][] = [];
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    for (let i = 0; i < N; i++) {
lib/sobolWorker.ts:        const rowA = [];
lib/sobolWorker.ts:        const rowB = [];
lib/sobolWorker.ts:        for (let j = 0; j < D; j++) {
lib/sobolWorker.ts:            rowA.push(randomNormal(inputMeans[j], inputStdDevs[j]));
lib/sobolWorker.ts:            rowB.push(randomNormal(inputMeans[j], inputStdDevs[j]));
lib/sobolWorker.ts:        }
lib/sobolWorker.ts:        A.push(rowA);
lib/sobolWorker.ts:        B.push(rowB);
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    }
lib/sobolWorker.ts:
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    // Evaluate yA and yB
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    const yA = new Float64Array(N);
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    const yB = new Float64Array(N);
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    for (let i = 0; i < N; i++) {
lib/sobolWorker.ts:        yA[i] = evaluate(A[i]);
lib/sobolWorker.ts:        yB[i] = evaluate(B[i]);
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    }
lib/sobolWorker.ts:
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    // Calculate Variance V(Y) from yA (and yB combined for better estimate)
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    let sum = 0;
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    let sumSq = 0;
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    for (let i = 0; i < N; i++) {
lib/sobolWorker.ts:        sum += yA[i] + yB[i];
lib/sobolWorker.ts:        sumSq += yA[i]*yA[i] + yB[i]*yB[i];
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    }
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    const meanY = sum / (2 * N);
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    const varY = sumSq / (2 * N) - meanY * meanY;
lib/sobolWorker.ts:
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    if (varY === 0) {
lib/sobolWorker.ts:        throw new Error("Variance of output is 0. Sensitivity indices cannot be calculated.");
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    }
lib/sobolWorker.ts:
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    const firstOrder = [];
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    const totalEffect = [];
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    const interactions = [];
lib/sobolWorker.ts:
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    // For each variable
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    for (let i = 0; i < D; i++) {
lib/sobolWorker.ts:        const yABi = new Float64Array(N);
lib/sobolWorker.ts:        for (let j = 0; j < N; j++) {
lib/sobolWorker.ts:            // A_B^(i): B's i-th column, A's other columns
lib/sobolWorker.ts:            const rowAB = [...A[j]];
lib/sobolWorker.ts:            rowAB[i] = B[j][i];
lib/sobolWorker.ts:            yABi[j] = evaluate(rowAB);
lib/sobolWorker.ts:        }
lib/sobolWorker.ts:
lib/sobolWorker.ts:        // Jansen estimator
lib/sobolWorker.ts:        let sumVti = 0;
lib/sobolWorker.ts:        let sumVi = 0;
lib/sobolWorker.ts:        for (let j = 0; j < N; j++) {
lib/sobolWorker.ts:            const diffA = yA[j] - yABi[j];
lib/sobolWorker.ts:            sumVti += diffA * diffA;
lib/sobolWorker.ts:
lib/sobolWorker.ts:            const diffB = yB[j] - yABi[j];
lib/sobolWorker.ts:            sumVi += diffB * diffB;
lib/sobolWorker.ts:        }
lib/sobolWorker.ts:
lib/sobolWorker.ts:        const vti = sumVti / (2 * N); // Total variance contribution
lib/sobolWorker.ts:        const vi = varY - (sumVi / (2 * N)); // First order variance contribution
lib/sobolWorker.ts:
lib/sobolWorker.ts:        const sTi = Math.max(0, vti / varY);
lib/sobolWorker.ts:        let sI = Math.max(0, vi / varY);
lib/sobolWorker.ts:        if (sI > sTi) sI = sTi; // Numeric precision guard
lib/sobolWorker.ts:
lib/sobolWorker.ts:        firstOrder.push({ name: inputKeys[i], value: sI });
lib/sobolWorker.ts:        totalEffect.push({ name: inputKeys[i], value: sTi });
lib/sobolWorker.ts:        interactions.push({ name: inputKeys[i], value: sTi - sI });
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    }
lib/sobolWorker.ts:
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    // Sort by Total Effect descending
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    const indices = Array.from({length: D}, (_, i) => i).sort((a, b) => totalEffect[b].value - totalEffect[a].value);
lib/sobolWorker.ts:
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    self.postMessage({
lib/sobolWorker.ts:      type: 'SOBOL_COMPLETE',
lib/sobolWorker.ts:      payload: {
lib/sobolWorker.ts:        firstOrder: indices.map(i => firstOrder[i]),
lib/sobolWorker.ts:        totalEffect: indices.map(i => totalEffect[i]),
lib/sobolWorker.ts:        interactions: indices.map(i => interactions[i])
lib/sobolWorker.ts:      }
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    });
lib/sobolWorker.ts:
lib/sobolWorker.ts:  } catch (error) {
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    self.postMessage({
lib/sobolWorker.ts:      type: 'ERROR',
lib/sobolWorker.ts:      error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)
lib/sobolWorker.ts:    });
lib/sobolWorker.ts:  }
lib/sobolWorker.ts:};
lib/math.ts:/**
lib/math.ts: * Statistical utility functions for data analysis.
lib/math.ts: */
lib/math.ts:
lib/math.ts:// Basic Linear Regression with safety checks
lib/math.ts:export const linearRegression = (data: { x: number; y: number }[]) => {
lib/math.ts:  const n = data.length;
lib/math.ts:  if (n < 2) return { fn: (_x: number) => 0, slope: 0, intercept: 0 };
lib/math.ts:  
lib/math.ts:  let sumX = 0,
lib/math.ts:    sumY = 0,
lib/math.ts:    sumXY = 0,
lib/math.ts:    sumXX = 0;
lib/math.ts:  for (const { x, y } of data) {
lib/math.ts:    sumX += x;
lib/math.ts:    sumY += y;
lib/math.ts:    sumXY += x * y;
lib/math.ts:    sumXX += x * x;
lib/math.ts:  }
lib/math.ts:  
lib/math.ts:  const denom = (n * sumXX - sumX * sumX);
lib/math.ts:  if (denom === 0) return { fn: (_x: number) => data[0].y, slope: 0, intercept: data[0].y };
lib/math.ts:  
lib/math.ts:  const slope = (n * sumXY - sumX * sumY) / denom;
lib/math.ts:  const intercept = (sumY - slope * sumX) / n;
lib/math.ts:  return { fn: (x: number) => slope * x + intercept, slope, intercept };
lib/math.ts:};
lib/math.ts:
lib/math.ts:/**
lib/math.ts: * Calculates mean, standard deviation, min, max, and count for a set of numbers.
lib/math.ts: */
lib/math.ts:export const calculateStats = (data: number[]) => {
lib/math.ts:  if (!data || data.length === 0)
lib/math.ts:    return { mean: 0, stdDev: 0, min: 0, max: 0, count: 0 };
lib/math.ts:
lib/math.ts:  const count = data.length;
lib/math.ts:  const mean = data.reduce((sum, val) => sum + val, 0) / count;
lib/math.ts:  const squareDiffs = data.map((value) => {
lib/math.ts:    const diff = value - mean;
lib/math.ts:    return diff * diff;
lib/math.ts:  });
lib/math.ts:  const stdDev = Math.sqrt(
lib/math.ts:    squareDiffs.reduce((sum, val) => sum + val, 0) / count,
lib/math.ts:  );
lib/math.ts:  const min = Math.min(...data);
lib/math.ts:  const max = Math.max(...data);
lib/math.ts:
lib/math.ts:  return { mean, stdDev, min, max, count };
lib/math.ts:};
lib/math.ts:
lib/math.ts:/**
lib/math.ts: * Calculates 95% confidence interval based on standard deviation and count.
lib/math.ts: */
lib/math.ts:export const getConfidenceInterval95 = (
lib/math.ts:  mean: number,
lib/math.ts:  stdDev: number,
lib/math.ts:  count: number,
lib/math.ts:) => {
lib/math.ts:  if (count <= 1) return { lower: mean, upper: mean };
lib/math.ts:  const z = 1.96;
lib/math.ts:  const marginOfError = z * (stdDev / Math.sqrt(count));
lib/math.ts:  return { lower: mean - marginOfError, upper: mean + marginOfError };
lib/math.ts:};
lib/paretoWorker.ts:export type ParetoObjective = {
lib/paretoWorker.ts:  key: string;
lib/paretoWorker.ts:  minimize: boolean;
lib/paretoWorker.ts:};
lib/paretoWorker.ts:
lib/paretoWorker.ts:export type ParetoMessage = {
lib/paretoWorker.ts:  type: 'COMPUTE_PARETO';
lib/paretoWorker.ts:  payload: {
lib/paretoWorker.ts:    data: { id: string; values: Record<string, number> }[];
lib/paretoWorker.ts:    objectives: ParetoObjective[];
lib/paretoWorker.ts:  };
lib/paretoWorker.ts:};
lib/paretoWorker.ts:
lib/paretoWorker.ts:export type ParetoResponse = {
lib/paretoWorker.ts:  type: 'PARETO_RESULT';
lib/paretoWorker.ts:  payload: {
lib/paretoWorker.ts:    paretoIds: string[];
lib/paretoWorker.ts:  };
lib/paretoWorker.ts:} | {
lib/paretoWorker.ts:  type: 'ERROR';
lib/paretoWorker.ts:  payload: { message: string };
lib/paretoWorker.ts:};
lib/paretoWorker.ts:
lib/paretoWorker.ts:// Dominance check for multi-dimensional pareto
lib/paretoWorker.ts:function dominates(a: number[], b: number[], minimize: boolean[]): boolean {
lib/paretoWorker.ts:  let strictlyBetter = false;
lib/paretoWorker.ts:  for (let i = 0; i < a.length; i++) {
lib/paretoWorker.ts:    if (minimize[i]) {
lib/paretoWorker.ts:      if (a[i] > b[i]) return false;
lib/paretoWorker.ts:      if (a[i] < b[i]) strictlyBetter = true;
lib/paretoWorker.ts:    } else {
lib/paretoWorker.ts:      if (a[i] < b[i]) return false;
lib/paretoWorker.ts:      if (a[i] > b[i]) strictlyBetter = true;
lib/paretoWorker.ts:    }
lib/paretoWorker.ts:  }
lib/paretoWorker.ts:  return strictlyBetter;
lib/paretoWorker.ts:}
lib/paretoWorker.ts:
lib/paretoWorker.ts:self.onmessage = (e: MessageEvent<ParetoMessage>) => {
lib/paretoWorker.ts:  try {
lib/paretoWorker.ts:    const { data, objectives } = e.data.payload;
lib/paretoWorker.ts:    if (!objectives.length || !data.length) {
lib/paretoWorker.ts:      self.postMessage({ type: 'PARETO_RESULT', payload: { paretoIds: data.map(d => d.id) } });
lib/paretoWorker.ts:      return;
lib/paretoWorker.ts:    }
lib/paretoWorker.ts:
lib/paretoWorker.ts:    const minimizeMap = objectives.map(o => o.minimize);
lib/paretoWorker.ts:    const keys = objectives.map(o => o.key);
lib/paretoWorker.ts:
lib/paretoWorker.ts:    const points = data.map(d => ({
lib/paretoWorker.ts:      id: d.id,
lib/paretoWorker.ts:      values: keys.map(k => d.values[k] ?? (minimizeMap[keys.indexOf(k)] ? Infinity : -Infinity))
lib/paretoWorker.ts:    }));
lib/paretoWorker.ts:
lib/paretoWorker.ts:    const paretoIds: string[] = [];
lib/paretoWorker.ts:
lib/paretoWorker.ts:    // Simple O(N^2) dominance check - for < 100,000 points this is often fast enough with early pruning
lib/paretoWorker.ts:    // For strictly 2 objectives, we could sort and do O(N log N)
lib/paretoWorker.ts:    if (objectives.length === 2) {
lib/paretoWorker.ts:       // O(N log N) sweep line
lib/paretoWorker.ts:       // Sort by primary objective (better first)
lib/paretoWorker.ts:       const o1Min = minimizeMap[0];
lib/paretoWorker.ts:       const o2Min = minimizeMap[1];
lib/paretoWorker.ts:       
lib/paretoWorker.ts:       points.sort((a, b) => {
lib/paretoWorker.ts:          if (a.values[0] !== b.values[0]) {
lib/paretoWorker.ts:             return o1Min ? a.values[0] - b.values[0] : b.values[0] - a.values[0];
lib/paretoWorker.ts:          }
lib/paretoWorker.ts:          return o2Min ? a.values[1] - b.values[1] : b.values[1] - a.values[1];
lib/paretoWorker.ts:       });
lib/paretoWorker.ts:
lib/paretoWorker.ts:       let bestO2SoFar = o2Min ? Infinity : -Infinity;
lib/paretoWorker.ts:       
lib/paretoWorker.ts:       for (const p of points) {
lib/paretoWorker.ts:           const v2 = p.values[1];
lib/paretoWorker.ts:           if (o2Min) {
lib/paretoWorker.ts:               if (v2 < bestO2SoFar) {
lib/paretoWorker.ts:                   paretoIds.push(p.id);
lib/paretoWorker.ts:                   bestO2SoFar = v2;
lib/paretoWorker.ts:               } else if (v2 === bestO2SoFar && p.values[0] === points[paretoIds.length > 0 ? points.findIndex(x => x.id === paretoIds[paretoIds.length-1]) : 0]?.values[0]) {
lib/paretoWorker.ts:                   // identical points
lib/paretoWorker.ts:                   paretoIds.push(p.id);
lib/paretoWorker.ts:               }
lib/paretoWorker.ts:           } else {
lib/paretoWorker.ts:               if (v2 > bestO2SoFar) {
lib/paretoWorker.ts:                   paretoIds.push(p.id);
lib/paretoWorker.ts:                   bestO2SoFar = v2;
lib/paretoWorker.ts:               } else if (v2 === bestO2SoFar && p.values[0] === points[paretoIds.length > 0 ? points.findIndex(x => x.id === paretoIds[paretoIds.length-1]) : 0]?.values[0]) {
lib/paretoWorker.ts:                   paretoIds.push(p.id);
lib/paretoWorker.ts:               }
lib/paretoWorker.ts:           }
lib/paretoWorker.ts:       }
lib/paretoWorker.ts:    } else {
lib/paretoWorker.ts:       // O(N^2)
lib/paretoWorker.ts:       for (let i = 0; i < points.length; i++) {
lib/paretoWorker.ts:         let dominated = false;
lib/paretoWorker.ts:         for (let j = 0; j < points.length; j++) {
lib/paretoWorker.ts:           if (i === j) continue;
lib/paretoWorker.ts:           if (dominates(points[j].values, points[i].values, minimizeMap)) {
lib/paretoWorker.ts:             dominated = true;
lib/paretoWorker.ts:             break;
lib/paretoWorker.ts:           }
lib/paretoWorker.ts:         }
lib/paretoWorker.ts:         if (!dominated) {
lib/paretoWorker.ts:           paretoIds.push(points[i].id);
lib/paretoWorker.ts:         }
lib/paretoWorker.ts:       }
lib/paretoWorker.ts:    }
lib/paretoWorker.ts:
lib/paretoWorker.ts:    self.postMessage({ type: 'PARETO_RESULT', payload: { paretoIds } } as ParetoResponse);
lib/paretoWorker.ts:  } catch (error) {
lib/paretoWorker.ts:    self.postMessage({ type: 'ERROR', payload: { message: error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Unknown error' } } as ParetoResponse);
lib/paretoWorker.ts:  }
lib/paretoWorker.ts:};
lib/utils.ts:import { type ClassValue, clsx } from "clsx";
lib/utils.ts:import { twMerge } from "tailwind-merge";
lib/utils.ts:
lib/utils.ts:export function cn(...inputs: ClassValue[]) {
lib/utils.ts:  return twMerge(clsx(inputs));
lib/utils.ts:}
lib/utils.ts:
lib/utils.ts:export function generateId(): string {
lib/utils.ts:  if (typeof crypto !== 'undefined' && crypto.randomUUID) {
lib/utils.ts:    return crypto.randomUUID();
lib/utils.ts:  }
lib/utils.ts:  return `p-${Math.random().toString(36).substring(2, 9)}`;
lib/utils.ts:}
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:import { FormulaConfig, Product, PropertyValue } from '../types';
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:import { formulaEngine } from './formulaParser';
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:export interface MonteCarloMessage {
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:  type: 'RUN_SIMULATION';
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:  payload: {
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    targetFormulaId: string;
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    formulas: FormulaConfig[];
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    product: Product;
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    variances: Record<string, number>; // std dev as % of base value (e.g., 5 for 5%)
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    iterations?: number;
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:  };
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:}
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:export interface MonteCarloResponse {
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:  type: 'SIMULATION_COMPLETE' | 'ERROR';
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:  payload?: {
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    results: number[]; // the array of outputs
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    stats: {
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:      mean: number;
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:      stdDev: number;
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:      p5: number;
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:      p95: number;
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:      kde: {x: number, y: number}[];
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    };
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:  };
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:  error?: string;
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:}
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:// Box-Muller transform for normal distribution sampling
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:function randomNormal(mean: number, stdDev: number): number {
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:  let u = 0, v = 0;
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:  while(u === 0) u = Math.random(); //Converting [0,1) to (0,1)
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:  while(v === 0) v = Math.random();
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:  const num = Math.sqrt( -2.0 * Math.log( u ) ) * Math.cos( 2.0 * Math.PI * v );
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:  return num * stdDev + mean;
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:}
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:// Kernel Density Estimation (Gaussian)
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:function calculateKDE(data: number[], bandwidth: number, steps: number = 100): {x: number, y: number}[] {
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    if (data.length === 0) return [];
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    let min = data[0], max = data[0];
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    for (const d of data) {
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:        if (d < min) min = d;
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:        if (d > max) max = d;
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    }
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    // Add margin
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    const margin = (max - min) * 0.1 || bandwidth * 3;
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    min -= margin;
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    max += margin;
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    const step = (max - min) / steps;
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    const kde = [];
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    for (let x = min; x <= max; x += step) {
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:        let sum = 0;
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:        for (const d of data) {
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:            const u = (x - d) / bandwidth;
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:            sum += Math.exp(-0.5 * u * u) / (Math.sqrt(2 * Math.PI));
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:        }
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:        kde.push({ x, y: sum / (data.length * bandwidth) });
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    }
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    return kde;
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:}
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:self.onmessage = (e: MessageEvent<MonteCarloMessage>) => {
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:  try {
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    const { targetFormulaId, formulas, product, variances, iterations = 5000 } = e.data.payload;
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    const evaluator = formulaEngine.compileGraph(formulas);
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    const results: number[] = [];
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    // Determine which properties actually need variance
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    const baseProperties = product.properties;
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    for (let i = 0; i < iterations; i++) {
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:        // Create a fake product with perturbed properties
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:        const perturbedProps: Record<string, PropertyValue> = {};
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:        
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:        for (const [key, val] of Object.entries(baseProperties)) {
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:            const numVal = parseFloat(String(val.value));
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:            if (!isNaN(numVal) && variances[key]) {
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:                const stdDev = numVal * (variances[key] / 100);
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:                perturbedProps[key] = { ...val, value: randomNormal(numVal, stdDev) };
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:            } else {
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:                perturbedProps[key] = val;
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:            }
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:        }
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:        
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:        const testProduct = { ...product, properties: perturbedProps } as Product;
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:        const computed = evaluator(testProduct);
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:        const result = computed[targetFormulaId];
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:        
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:        if (result !== undefined && !isNaN(result)) {
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:            results.push(result);
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:        }
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    }
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    if (results.length === 0) {
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:        throw new Error("Simulation yielded no valid numeric results.");
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    }
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    results.sort((a, b) => a - b);
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    const sum = results.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0);
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    const mean = sum / results.length;
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    const variance = results.reduce((a, b) => a + Math.pow(b - mean, 2), 0) / results.length;
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    const stdDev = Math.sqrt(variance);
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    const p5 = results[Math.floor(results.length * 0.05)];
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    const p95 = results[Math.floor(results.length * 0.95)];
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    // Scott's rule for bandwidth
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    const bandwidth = 1.06 * stdDev * Math.pow(results.length, -0.2);
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    const kde = calculateKDE(results, bandwidth, 100);
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    self.postMessage({
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:      type: 'SIMULATION_COMPLETE',
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:      payload: {
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:        results,
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:        stats: { mean, stdDev, p5, p95, kde }
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:      }
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    });
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:  } catch (error) {
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    self.postMessage({
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:      type: 'ERROR',
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:      error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:    });
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:  }
lib/monteCarloWorker.ts:};
lib/materialScience.ts:/**
lib/materialScience.ts: * Material Scientific Engine
lib/materialScience.ts: * Handles normalization, correlation analysis, and expert insight generation
lib/materialScience.ts: * specifically for polymer and chemical database analysis.
lib/materialScience.ts: */
lib/materialScience.ts:
lib/materialScience.ts:export interface CorrelationResult {
lib/materialScience.ts:  propertyX: string;
lib/materialScience.ts:  propertyY: string;
lib/materialScience.ts:  r2: number;
lib/materialScience.ts:  slope: number;
lib/materialScience.ts:  intercept: number;
lib/materialScience.ts:  trend: 'positive' | 'negative' | 'none';
lib/materialScience.ts:}
lib/materialScience.ts:
lib/materialScience.ts:export interface Insight {
lib/materialScience.ts:  title: string;
lib/materialScience.ts:  content: string;
lib/materialScience.ts:  type: 'info' | 'warning' | 'success';
lib/materialScience.ts:}
lib/materialScience.ts:
lib/materialScience.ts:export const materialEngine = {
lib/materialScience.ts:  /**
lib/materialScience.ts:   * Percentile-based Normalization
lib/materialScience.ts:   * Maps a value to its rank in the current dataset (0-100)
lib/materialScience.ts:   * This is the BEST way to show relative strengths regardless of units.
lib/materialScience.ts:   */
lib/materialScience.ts:  normalizePercentile: (val: number, dataArray: number[]): number => {
lib/materialScience.ts:    if (dataArray.length < 2) return 50;
lib/materialScience.ts:    const sorted = [...dataArray].sort((a, b) => a - b);
lib/materialScience.ts:    const count = sorted.filter(v => v < val).length;
lib/materialScience.ts:    // Stretch the distribution to use the full 20-100 range for visual clarity
lib/materialScience.ts:    const percentile = (count / (sorted.length - 1)) * 80 + 20;
lib/materialScience.ts:    return Math.max(5, Math.min(100, isNaN(percentile) ? 50 : percentile));
lib/materialScience.ts:  },
lib/materialScience.ts:
lib/materialScience.ts:  /**
lib/materialScience.ts:   * Calculates smart axis bounds with 10% padding
lib/materialScience.ts:   */
lib/materialScience.ts:  calculateBounds: (dataArray: number[], isLog: boolean = false) => {
lib/materialScience.ts:    const valid = dataArray.filter(v => v > 0 && !isNaN(v) && isFinite(v));
lib/materialScience.ts:    if (valid.length === 0) return { min: isLog ? 0.1 : 0, max: 100 };
lib/materialScience.ts:    
lib/materialScience.ts:    const min = Math.min(...valid);
lib/materialScience.ts:    const max = Math.max(...valid);
lib/materialScience.ts:    
lib/materialScience.ts:    if (min === max) {
lib/materialScience.ts:      if (isLog) return { min: min * 0.1, max: min * 10 };
lib/materialScience.ts:      return { min: min * 0.9, max: max * 1.1 };
lib/materialScience.ts:    }
lib/materialScience.ts:
lib/materialScience.ts:    const margin = (max - min) * 0.15;
lib/materialScience.ts:    
lib/materialScience.ts:    if (isLog) {
lib/materialScience.ts:      const logMin = Math.log10(min);
lib/materialScience.ts:      const logMax = Math.log10(max);
lib/materialScience.ts:      const logMargin = (logMax - logMin) * 0.15;
lib/materialScience.ts:      return { 
lib/materialScience.ts:        min: Math.pow(10, logMin - logMargin), 
lib/materialScience.ts:        max: Math.pow(10, logMax + logMargin) 
lib/materialScience.ts:      };
lib/materialScience.ts:    }
lib/materialScience.ts:    return { 
lib/materialScience.ts:      min: Math.max(0, min - margin), 
lib/materialScience.ts:      max: max + margin 
lib/materialScience.ts:    };
lib/materialScience.ts:  },
lib/materialScience.ts:
lib/materialScience.ts:  /**
lib/materialScience.ts:   * Simple Linear Regression for Trend Detection
lib/materialScience.ts:   */
lib/materialScience.ts:  analyzeCorrelation: (points: [number, number][]): CorrelationResult | null => {
lib/materialScience.ts:    if (points.length < 2) return null;
lib/materialScience.ts:    
lib/materialScience.ts:    let sumX = 0, sumY = 0, sumXY = 0, sumX2 = 0, sumY2 = 0;
lib/materialScience.ts:    const n = points.length;
lib/materialScience.ts:
lib/materialScience.ts:    for (const [x, y] of points) {
lib/materialScience.ts:      sumX += x;
lib/materialScience.ts:      sumY += y;
lib/materialScience.ts:      sumXY += x * y;
lib/materialScience.ts:      sumX2 += x * x;
lib/materialScience.ts:      sumY2 += y * y;
lib/materialScience.ts:    }
lib/materialScience.ts:
lib/materialScience.ts:    const numerator = n * sumXY - sumX * sumY;
lib/materialScience.ts:    const denominator = Math.sqrt((n * sumX2 - sumX * sumX) * (n * sumY2 - sumY * sumY));
lib/materialScience.ts:    
lib/materialScience.ts:    if (denominator === 0) return null;
lib/materialScience.ts:    
lib/materialScience.ts:    const r = numerator / denominator;
lib/materialScience.ts:    const r2 = r * r;
lib/materialScience.ts:    const slope = (n * sumXY - sumX * sumY) / (n * sumX2 - sumX * sumX);
lib/materialScience.ts:    const intercept = (sumY - slope * sumX) / n;
lib/materialScience.ts:
lib/materialScience.ts:    return {
lib/materialScience.ts:      propertyX: '',
lib/materialScience.ts:      propertyY: '',
lib/materialScience.ts:      r2,
lib/materialScience.ts:      slope,
lib/materialScience.ts:      intercept,
lib/materialScience.ts:      trend: r > 0.3 ? 'positive' : r < -0.3 ? 'negative' : 'none'
lib/materialScience.ts:    };
lib/materialScience.ts:  },
lib/materialScience.ts:
lib/materialScience.ts:  /**
lib/materialScience.ts:   * Performs Linear Regression in Log-Log space for Ashby Plots
lib/materialScience.ts:   * Fits y = C * x^k (Power Law Relationship)
lib/materialScience.ts:   */
lib/materialScience.ts:  analyzeCorrelationLog: (points: [number, number][]) => {
lib/materialScience.ts:    const logPoints = points
lib/materialScience.ts:      .filter(([x, y]) => x > 0 && y > 0)
lib/materialScience.ts:      .map(([x, y]) => [Math.log10(x), Math.log10(y)] as [number, number]);
lib/materialScience.ts:    
lib/materialScience.ts:    if (logPoints.length < 2) return null;
lib/materialScience.ts:    const linearModel = materialEngine.analyzeCorrelation(logPoints);
lib/materialScience.ts:    if (!linearModel) return null;
lib/materialScience.ts:
lib/materialScience.ts:    return {
lib/materialScience.ts:      r2: linearModel.r2,
lib/materialScience.ts:      k: linearModel.slope, // Power exponent
lib/materialScience.ts:      c: Math.pow(10, linearModel.intercept), // Pre-exponential factor
lib/materialScience.ts:      regressionFn: (x: number) => Math.pow(10, linearModel.intercept) * Math.pow(x, linearModel.slope)
lib/materialScience.ts:    };
lib/materialScience.ts:  },
lib/materialScience.ts:
lib/materialScience.ts:  /**
lib/materialScience.ts:   * Calculates the Pearson Correlation Coefficient (r)
lib/materialScience.ts:   */
lib/materialScience.ts:  calculatePearson: (points: [number, number][]): number => {
lib/materialScience.ts:    if (points.length < 2) return 0;
lib/materialScience.ts:    const n = points.length;
lib/materialScience.ts:    let sumX = 0, sumY = 0, sumXY = 0, sumX2 = 0, sumY2 = 0;
lib/materialScience.ts:    for (const [x, y] of points) {
lib/materialScience.ts:      sumX += x;
lib/materialScience.ts:      sumY += y;
lib/materialScience.ts:      sumXY += x * y;
lib/materialScience.ts:      sumX2 += x * x;
lib/materialScience.ts:      sumY2 += y * y;
lib/materialScience.ts:    }
lib/materialScience.ts:    const num = n * sumXY - sumX * sumY;
lib/materialScience.ts:    const den = Math.sqrt((n * sumX2 - sumX * sumX) * (n * sumY2 - sumY * sumY));
lib/materialScience.ts:    return den === 0 ? 0 : num / den;
lib/materialScience.ts:  },
lib/materialScience.ts:
lib/materialScience.ts:  /**
lib/materialScience.ts:   * Calculates Polymer Molecular Weight Moments (Mn, Mw, Mz, PDI)
lib/materialScience.ts:   * Essential for GPC analysis
lib/materialScience.ts:   */
lib/materialScience.ts:  calculateMWDMoments: (mwd: { x: number; y: number }[]) => {
lib/materialScience.ts:    if (mwd.length < 2) return null;
lib/materialScience.ts:    let sumNi = 0;
lib/materialScience.ts:    let sumNiMi = 0;
lib/materialScience.ts:    let sumNiMi2 = 0;
lib/materialScience.ts:    let sumNiMi3 = 0;
lib/materialScience.ts:
lib/materialScience.ts:    for (const p of mwd) {
lib/materialScience.ts:      if (p.x <= 0 || p.y <= 0) continue;
lib/materialScience.ts:      const ni = p.y; // Signal intensity counts as relative abundance
lib/materialScience.ts:      const mi = p.x; // Molecular Weight
lib/materialScience.ts:      sumNi += ni;
lib/materialScience.ts:      sumNiMi += ni * mi;
lib/materialScience.ts:      sumNiMi2 += ni * Math.pow(mi, 2);
lib/materialScience.ts:      sumNiMi3 += ni * Math.pow(mi, 3);
lib/materialScience.ts:    }
lib/materialScience.ts:
lib/materialScience.ts:    if (sumNi === 0 || sumNiMi === 0) return null;
lib/materialScience.ts:
lib/materialScience.ts:    const mn = sumNiMi / sumNi;
lib/materialScience.ts:    const mw = sumNiMi2 / sumNiMi;
lib/materialScience.ts:    const mz = sumNiMi3 / sumNiMi2;
lib/materialScience.ts:    const pdi = mw / mn;
lib/materialScience.ts:
lib/materialScience.ts:    return { mn, mw, mz, pdi };
lib/materialScience.ts:  },
lib/materialScience.ts:
lib/materialScience.ts:  /**
lib/materialScience.ts:   * Calculates the Power Law Index (n) and Consistency Index (K)
lib/materialScience.ts:   * for Non-Newtonian Fluid Rheology (Shear Thinning Behavior)
lib/materialScience.ts:   */
lib/materialScience.ts:  calculatePowerLawIndex: (points: { rate: number; stress: number }[]) => {
lib/materialScience.ts:    const logPoints = points
lib/materialScience.ts:      .filter(p => p.rate > 0 && p.stress > 0)
lib/materialScience.ts:      .map(p => ({ x: Math.log10(p.rate), y: Math.log10(p.stress) }));
lib/materialScience.ts:    
lib/materialScience.ts:    if (logPoints.length < 2) return null;
lib/materialScience.ts:    // Regression on log-log data
lib/materialScience.ts:    const n = points.length;
lib/materialScience.ts:    let sumX = 0, sumY = 0, sumXY = 0, sumXX = 0;
lib/materialScience.ts:    for (const p of logPoints) {
lib/materialScience.ts:      sumX += p.x;
lib/materialScience.ts:      sumY += p.y;
lib/materialScience.ts:      sumXY += p.x * p.y;
lib/materialScience.ts:      sumXX += p.x * p.x;
lib/materialScience.ts:    }
lib/materialScience.ts:    const slope = (n * sumXY - sumX * sumY) / (n * sumXX - sumX * sumX);
lib/materialScience.ts:    const intercept = (sumY - slope * sumX) / n;
lib/materialScience.ts:    
lib/materialScience.ts:    return {
lib/materialScience.ts:      n: slope, // Flow behavior index
lib/materialScience.ts:      k: Math.pow(10, intercept), // Consistency index
lib/materialScience.ts:      isShearThinning: slope < 1
lib/materialScience.ts:    };
lib/materialScience.ts:  },
lib/materialScience.ts:
lib/materialScience.ts:  /**
lib/materialScience.ts:   * Robust Correlation Analysis
lib/materialScience.ts:   * Compares Pearson (linear) vs Spearman (monotonic)
lib/materialScience.ts:   * Better for non-linear but monotonic relationships
lib/materialScience.ts:   */
lib/materialScience.ts:  calculateSpearman: (points: [number, number][]): number => {
lib/materialScience.ts:    if (points.length < 2) return 0;
lib/materialScience.ts:    const n = points.length;
lib/materialScience.ts:    
lib/materialScience.ts:    // Helper to get ranks
lib/materialScience.ts:    const getRanks = (vals: number[]) => {
lib/materialScience.ts:      const sorted = vals.map((v, i) => ({ v, i })).sort((a, b) => a.v - b.v);
lib/materialScience.ts:      const ranks = new Array(n);
lib/materialScience.ts:      for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) {
lib/materialScience.ts:        ranks[sorted[i].i] = i + 1;
lib/materialScience.ts:      }
lib/materialScience.ts:      return ranks;
lib/materialScience.ts:    };
lib/materialScience.ts:
lib/materialScience.ts:    const xRanks = getRanks(points.map(p => p[0]));
lib/materialScience.ts:    const yRanks = getRanks(points.map(p => p[1]));
lib/materialScience.ts:
lib/materialScience.ts:    let dSqSum = 0;
lib/materialScience.ts:    for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) {
lib/materialScience.ts:      dSqSum += Math.pow(xRanks[i] - yRanks[i], 2);
lib/materialScience.ts:    }
lib/materialScience.ts:
lib/materialScience.ts:    return 1 - (6 * dSqSum) / (n * (Math.pow(n, 2) - 1));
lib/materialScience.ts:  },
lib/materialScience.ts:
lib/materialScience.ts:  /**
lib/materialScience.ts:   * Estimating Zero-Shear Viscosity (eta0) using Cross Model approximation
lib/materialScience.ts:   * 聚合物熔体零剪切粘度推算
lib/materialScience.ts:   */
lib/materialScience.ts:  estimateZeroShearViscosity: (points: { rate: number; viscosity: number }[]) => {
lib/materialScience.ts:    if (points.length < 3) return null;
lib/materialScience.ts:    // Simple heuristic: average of the 3 lowest shear rate points if they are in the plateau
lib/materialScience.ts:    const sorted = [...points].sort((a, b) => a.rate - b.rate);
lib/materialScience.ts:    const lowShear = sorted.slice(0, Math.min(3, sorted.length));
lib/materialScience.ts:    const avgVisc = lowShear.reduce((a, b) => a + b.viscosity, 0) / lowShear.length;
lib/materialScience.ts:    
lib/materialScience.ts:    // Check if we are actually in the plateau (slope near 0)
lib/materialScience.ts:    const slope = (lowShear[lowShear.length - 1].viscosity - lowShear[0].viscosity) / 
lib/materialScience.ts:                  (lowShear[lowShear.length - 1].rate - lowShear[0].rate || 1);
lib/materialScience.ts:    
lib/materialScience.ts:    return {
lib/materialScience.ts:      value: avgVisc,
lib/materialScience.ts:      isReliable: Math.abs(slope) < avgVisc * 0.1 // Slope is small relative to magnitude
lib/materialScience.ts:    };
lib/materialScience.ts:  },
lib/materialScience.ts:
lib/materialScience.ts:  /**
lib/materialScience.ts:   * Robust Outlier Detection using Interquartile Range (IQR)
lib/materialScience.ts:   * 相比 Z-Score 更适用于非正态分布的实验数据
lib/materialScience.ts:   */
lib/materialScience.ts:  findOutliersIQR: (values: number[]): number[] => {
lib/materialScience.ts:    if (values.length < 4) return [];
lib/materialScience.ts:    const sorted = [...values].sort((a, b) => a - b);
lib/materialScience.ts:    const q1 = sorted[Math.floor(sorted.length * 0.25)];
lib/materialScience.ts:    const q3 = sorted[Math.floor(sorted.length * 0.75)];
lib/materialScience.ts:    const iqr = q3 - q1;
lib/materialScience.ts:    const lowerBound = q1 - 1.5 * iqr;
lib/materialScience.ts:    const upperBound = q3 + 1.5 * iqr;
lib/materialScience.ts:    return values.filter(v => v < lowerBound || v > upperBound);
lib/materialScience.ts:  },
lib/materialScience.ts:
lib/materialScience.ts:  /**
lib/materialScience.ts:   * Calculates a Weighted Performance Index (PI) for benchmarking
lib/materialScience.ts:   * 基于多维空间的性能平衡度评分
lib/materialScience.ts:   */
lib/materialScience.ts:  calculatePerformanceIndex: (fingerprint: number[]): number => {
lib/materialScience.ts:    if (fingerprint.length === 0) return 0;
lib/materialScience.ts:    // Use harmonic mean for PI to penalize extreme weaknesses (bottleneck effect)
lib/materialScience.ts:    const validValues = fingerprint.filter(v => v > 0);
lib/materialScience.ts:    if (validValues.length === 0) return 0;
lib/materialScience.ts:    const inverseSum = validValues.reduce((acc, v) => acc + (1 / v), 0);
lib/materialScience.ts:    return (validValues.length / inverseSum) * 10; // Normalized to 0-100 range
lib/materialScience.ts:  },
lib/materialScience.ts:
lib/materialScience.ts:  /**
lib/materialScience.ts:   * Performs basic descriptive statistics on a numeric property
lib/materialScience.ts:   */
lib/materialScience.ts:  getStats: (values: number[]) => {
lib/materialScience.ts:    if (values.length === 0) return null;
lib/materialScience.ts:    const sorted = [...values].sort((a, b) => a - b);
lib/materialScience.ts:    const mean = values.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / values.length;
lib/materialScience.ts:    const median = sorted[Math.floor(sorted.length / 2)];
lib/materialScience.ts:    const variance = values.reduce((a, b) => a + Math.pow(b - mean, 2), 0) / values.length;
lib/materialScience.ts:    
lib/materialScience.ts:    // IQR Calculation
lib/materialScience.ts:    const q1 = sorted[Math.floor(sorted.length * 0.25)];
lib/materialScience.ts:    const q3 = sorted[Math.floor(sorted.length * 0.75)];
lib/materialScience.ts:    const iqr = q3 - q1;
lib/materialScience.ts:
lib/materialScience.ts:    return {
lib/materialScience.ts:      min: sorted[0],
lib/materialScience.ts:      max: sorted[sorted.length - 1],
lib/materialScience.ts:      mean,
lib/materialScience.ts:      median,
lib/materialScience.ts:      stdDev: Math.sqrt(variance),
lib/materialScience.ts:      iqr,
lib/materialScience.ts:      q1,
lib/materialScience.ts:      q3,
lib/materialScience.ts:      range: sorted[sorted.length - 1] - sorted[0]
lib/materialScience.ts:    };
lib/materialScience.ts:  },
lib/materialScience.ts:  getParetoFrontier: (points: [number, number, string][]): [number, number, string][] => {
lib/materialScience.ts:    if (points.length === 0) return [];
lib/materialScience.ts:    // Sort x descending, then y descending
lib/materialScience.ts:    const sorted = [...points].sort((a, b) => b[0] - a[0] || b[1] - a[1]);
lib/materialScience.ts:    const frontier: [number, number, string][] = [];
lib/materialScience.ts:    let maxSoFar = -Infinity;
lib/materialScience.ts:
lib/materialScience.ts:    for (const p of sorted) {
lib/materialScience.ts:      if (p[1] > maxSoFar) {
lib/materialScience.ts:        frontier.push(p);
lib/materialScience.ts:        maxSoFar = p[1];
lib/materialScience.ts:      }
lib/materialScience.ts:    }
lib/materialScience.ts:    return frontier;
lib/materialScience.ts:  },
lib/materialScience.ts:
lib/materialScience.ts:  /**
lib/materialScience.ts:   * Multivariate Similarity Score (0-1)
lib/materialScience.ts:   * Calculates how similar two materials are based on shared numeric properties
lib/materialScience.ts:   */
lib/materialScience.ts:  calculateSimilarity: (v1: number[], v2: number[]): number => {
lib/materialScience.ts:    if (v1.length === 0 || v1.length !== v2.length) return 0;
lib/materialScience.ts:    let distSq = 0;
lib/materialScience.ts:    for (let i = 0; i < v1.length; i++) {
lib/materialScience.ts:      distSq += Math.pow(v1[i] - v2[i], 2);
lib/materialScience.ts:    }
lib/materialScience.ts:    const maxDist = Math.sqrt(v1.length * 100 * 100);
lib/materialScience.ts:    return Math.max(0, 1 - Math.sqrt(distSq) / maxDist);
lib/materialScience.ts:  },
lib/materialScience.ts:
lib/materialScience.ts:  /**
lib/materialScience.ts:   * Calculates Skewness and Kurtosis for a population
lib/materialScience.ts:   * Useful for understanding property distribution (asymmetry and tailedness)
lib/materialScience.ts:   */
lib/materialScience.ts:  calculateDistributionMoments: (values: number[]) => {
lib/materialScience.ts:    if (values.length < 3) return null;
lib/materialScience.ts:    const n = values.length;
lib/materialScience.ts:    const mean = values.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / n;
lib/materialScience.ts:    const stdDev = Math.sqrt(values.reduce((a, b) => a + Math.pow(b - mean, 2), 0) / n);
lib/materialScience.ts:    
lib/materialScience.ts:    if (stdDev === 0) return { skewness: 0, kurtosis: 0 };
lib/materialScience.ts:
lib/materialScience.ts:    let skewSum = 0;
lib/materialScience.ts:    let kurtSum = 0;
lib/materialScience.ts:    for (const v of values) {
lib/materialScience.ts:      const z = (v - mean) / stdDev;
lib/materialScience.ts:      skewSum += Math.pow(z, 3);
lib/materialScience.ts:      kurtSum += Math.pow(z, 4);
lib/materialScience.ts:    }
lib/materialScience.ts:
lib/materialScience.ts:    const skewness = (n / ((n - 1) * (n - 2))) * skewSum;
lib/materialScience.ts:    const kurtosis = (n * (n + 1) / ((n - 1) * (n - 2) * (n - 3))) * kurtSum - (3 * Math.pow(n - 1, 2) / ((n - 2) * (n - 3)));
lib/materialScience.ts:
lib/materialScience.ts:    return { skewness, kurtosis };
lib/materialScience.ts:  },
lib/materialScience.ts:
lib/materialScience.ts:  /**
lib/materialScience.ts:   * Detects outliers using the Z-Score method (Threshold = 3)
lib/materialScience.ts:   */
lib/materialScience.ts:  findOutliers: (values: number[], threshold: number = 3): number[] => {
lib/materialScience.ts:    if (values.length < 2) return [];
lib/materialScience.ts:    const mean = values.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / values.length;
lib/materialScience.ts:    const variance = values.reduce((a, b) => a + Math.pow(b - mean, 2), 0) / values.length;
lib/materialScience.ts:    const stdDev = Math.sqrt(variance);
lib/materialScience.ts:    
lib/materialScience.ts:    if (stdDev === 0) return [];
lib/materialScience.ts:    
lib/materialScience.ts:    return values.filter(v => Math.abs((v - mean) / stdDev) > threshold);
lib/materialScience.ts:  },
lib/materialScience.ts:
lib/materialScience.ts:  /**
lib/materialScience.ts:   * Calculates the 95% Confidence Interval for the slope of a regression line
lib/materialScience.ts:   */
lib/materialScience.ts:  calculateCI: (points: [number, number][], slope: number, intercept: number) => {
lib/materialScience.ts:    if (points.length < 3) return null;
lib/materialScience.ts:    const n = points.length;
lib/materialScience.ts:    let sumResidualSq = 0;
lib/materialScience.ts:    let sumX = 0;
lib/materialScience.ts:    let sumX2 = 0;
lib/materialScience.ts:
lib/materialScience.ts:    for (const [x, y] of points) {
lib/materialScience.ts:      const yPred = slope * x + intercept;
lib/materialScience.ts:      sumResidualSq += Math.pow(y - yPred, 2);
lib/materialScience.ts:      sumX += x;
lib/materialScience.ts:      sumX2 += x * x;
lib/materialScience.ts:    }
lib/materialScience.ts:
lib/materialScience.ts:    const seY = Math.sqrt(sumResidualSq / (n - 2));
lib/materialScience.ts:    const ssX = sumX2 - (Math.pow(sumX, 2) / n);
lib/materialScience.ts:    const seSlope = seY / Math.sqrt(ssX);
lib/materialScience.ts:    
lib/materialScience.ts:    // Simplification of t-value for 95% CI (approx 1.96 for n > 30, but we use 2.0 for safety)
lib/materialScience.ts:    const tValue = 2.0; 
lib/materialScience.ts:    return {
lib/materialScience.ts:      lower: slope - tValue * seSlope,
lib/materialScience.ts:      upper: slope + tValue * seSlope,
lib/materialScience.ts:      se: seSlope
lib/materialScience.ts:    };
lib/materialScience.ts:  },
lib/materialScience.ts:
lib/materialScience.ts:  /**
lib/materialScience.ts:   * Calculates the P-value for a given Pearson correlation r and sample size n
lib/materialScience.ts:   * (Simplification using t-distribution approximation)
lib/materialScience.ts:   */
lib/materialScience.ts:  calculatePValue: (r: number, n: number): number => {
lib/materialScience.ts:    if (n <= 2) return 1;
lib/materialScience.ts:    const t = Math.abs(r) * Math.sqrt((n - 2) / (1 - r * r));
lib/materialScience.ts:    // Approximation for P-value (Normal distribution CDF)
lib/materialScience.ts:    const erf = (x: number) => {
lib/materialScience.ts:      const a1 = 0.254829592, a2 = -0.284496736, a3 = 1.421413741, a4 = -1.453152027, a5 = 1.061405429, p = 0.3275911;
lib/materialScience.ts:      const t = 1.0 / (1.0 + p * Math.abs(x));
lib/materialScience.ts:      const y = 1.0 - (((((a5 * t + a4) * t) + a3) * t + a2) * t + a1) * t * Math.exp(-x * x);
lib/materialScience.ts:      return x >= 0 ? y : -y;
lib/materialScience.ts:    };
lib/materialScience.ts:    const cdf = 0.5 * (1 + erf(t / Math.sqrt(2)));
lib/materialScience.ts:    return 2 * (1 - cdf); // Two-tailed
lib/materialScience.ts:  },
lib/materialScience.ts:
lib/materialScience.ts:  /**
lib/materialScience.ts:   * Calculates Quartiles (Q1, Q2/Median, Q3) and Interquartile Range (IQR)
lib/materialScience.ts:   */
lib/materialScience.ts:  calculateQuartiles: (values: number[]) => {
lib/materialScience.ts:    if (values.length === 0) return null;
lib/materialScience.ts:    const sorted = [...values].sort((a, b) => a - b);
lib/materialScience.ts:    const getPercentile = (p: number) => {
lib/materialScience.ts:      const idx = (sorted.length - 1) * p;
lib/materialScience.ts:      const base = Math.floor(idx);
lib/materialScience.ts:      const rest = idx - base;
lib/materialScience.ts:      if (sorted[base + 1] !== undefined) {
lib/materialScience.ts:        return sorted[base] + rest * (sorted[base + 1] - sorted[base]);
lib/materialScience.ts:      }
lib/materialScience.ts:      return sorted[base];
lib/materialScience.ts:    };
lib/materialScience.ts:    const q1 = getPercentile(0.25);
lib/materialScience.ts:    const q2 = getPercentile(0.5);
lib/materialScience.ts:    const q3 = getPercentile(0.75);
lib/materialScience.ts:    return { q1, q2, q3, iqr: q3 - q1 };
lib/materialScience.ts:  },
lib/materialScience.ts:
lib/materialScience.ts:  /**
lib/materialScience.ts:   * Robust Regression Assessment (Cook's Distance Approximation)
lib/materialScience.ts:   * Measures the influence of each point on the model.
lib/materialScience.ts:   */
lib/materialScience.ts:  analyzeDatalineIntegrity: (points: [number, number][], slope: number, intercept: number) => {
lib/materialScience.ts:    if (points.length < 4) return { healthScore: 100, influentialPoints: [] };
lib/materialScience.ts:    const n = points.length;
lib/materialScience.ts:    let ssE = 0;
lib/materialScience.ts:    const hValues: number[] = [];
lib/materialScience.ts:    const meanX = points.reduce((a, b) => a + b[0], 0) / n;
lib/materialScience.ts:    const ssX = points.reduce((a, b) => a + Math.pow(b[0] - meanX, 2), 0);
lib/materialScience.ts:
lib/materialScience.ts:    points.forEach(([x, y]) => {
lib/materialScience.ts:      const yPred = slope * x + intercept;
lib/materialScience.ts:      ssE += Math.pow(y - yPred, 2);
lib/materialScience.ts:      hValues.push(1 / n + Math.pow(x - meanX, 2) / ssX);
lib/materialScience.ts:    });
lib/materialScience.ts:
lib/materialScience.ts:    const mse = ssE / (n - 2);
lib/materialScience.ts:    const influentialIndices: number[] = [];
lib/materialScience.ts:    
lib/materialScience.ts:    hValues.forEach((h, i) => {
lib/materialScience.ts:      const [x, y] = points[i];
lib/materialScience.ts:      const yPred = slope * x + intercept;
lib/materialScience.ts:      const residual = y - yPred;
lib/materialScience.ts:      // Cook's Distance approximation
lib/materialScience.ts:      const d = (Math.pow(residual, 2) / (2 * mse)) * (h / Math.pow(1 - h, 2));
lib/materialScience.ts:      if (d > 4 / n) influentialIndices.push(i);
lib/materialScience.ts:    });
lib/materialScience.ts:
lib/materialScience.ts:    return {
lib/materialScience.ts:      healthScore: Math.max(0, 100 - influentialIndices.length * (200 / n)),
lib/materialScience.ts:      influentialPointsCount: influentialIndices.length
lib/materialScience.ts:    };
lib/materialScience.ts:  },
lib/materialScience.ts:
lib/materialScience.ts:  /**
lib/materialScience.ts:   * Multidimensional Radar Scaling Logic
lib/materialScience.ts:   * Scales values based on historical or population distribution rather than simple max
lib/materialScience.ts:   */
lib/materialScience.ts:  getParetoPoints: (points: { x: number; y: number; name: string }[]): string[] => {
lib/materialScience.ts:    if (points.length === 0) return [];
lib/materialScience.ts:    const sorted = [...points].sort((a, b) => b.x - a.x || b.y - a.y);
lib/materialScience.ts:    const result: string[] = [];
lib/materialScience.ts:    let maxY = -Infinity;
lib/materialScience.ts:
lib/materialScience.ts:    for (const p of sorted) {
lib/materialScience.ts:      if (p.y > maxY) {
lib/materialScience.ts:        result.push(p.name);
lib/materialScience.ts:        maxY = p.y;
lib/materialScience.ts:      }
lib/materialScience.ts:    }
lib/materialScience.ts:    return result;
lib/materialScience.ts:  },
lib/materialScience.ts:
lib/materialScience.ts:  /**
lib/materialScience.ts:   * Formats statistical regression results for UI display
lib/materialScience.ts:   */
lib/materialScience.ts:  getRegressionLatex: (corr: CorrelationResult | null): string => {
lib/materialScience.ts:    if (!corr || isNaN(corr.r2)) return "";
lib/materialScience.ts:    const sign = corr.slope >= 0 ? "+" : "-";
lib/materialScience.ts:    return `y = ${corr.slope.toFixed(4)}x ${sign} ${Math.abs(corr.intercept).toFixed(2)} (R² = ${corr.r2.toFixed(3)})`;
lib/materialScience.ts:  },

lib/materialScience.ts:    switch (chartType) {
